allies.rationale_text: Risk-vs-uncertainty gives aleatory-epistemic-uncertainty-recognition a nearby decision surface for separating measurable risk from unresolved ignorance.
allies.source_quote: different forms of decision-making and analysis are required for each type

allies.rationale_text: Base-rates helps aleatory-epistemic-uncertainty-recognition discipline the inside view with outside-view statistical grounding.
allies.source_quote: anchoring decisions in the outside view

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias prevents aleatory-epistemic-uncertainty-recognition from updating ignorance honestly because disconfirming evidence never changes the uncertainty map.
antagonists.source_quote: prevents recognition of disconfirming evidence needed to reduce epistemic ignorance

structured_tensions.tension_text: Aleatory-epistemic-uncertainty-recognition conflicts with commitment-bias when teams persist with single-path plans despite unresolved unknowns.
structured_tensions.source_quote: aleatory-epistemic-uncertainty-recognition vs commitment-bias: aleatory-epistemic-uncertainty-recognition conflicts with commitment-bias when teams persist with single-path plans despite unresolved unknowns.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Aleatory-epistemic-uncertainty-recognition conflicts with status-quo-bias when default continuation replaces uncertainty-aware contingencies.
structured_tensions.source_quote: aleatory-epistemic-uncertainty-recognition vs status-quo-bias: aleatory-epistemic-uncertainty-recognition conflicts with status-quo-bias when default continuation replaces uncertainty-aware contingencies.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Aleatory-epistemic-uncertainty-recognition conflicts with confirmation-bias when the tendency to confirm existing beliefs prevents recognition of disconfirming evidence needed to reduce epistemic ignorance.
structured_tensions.source_quote: aleatory-epistemic-uncertainty-recognition vs confirmation-bias: aleatory-epistemic-uncertainty-recognition conflicts with confirmation-bias when the tendency to confirm existing beliefs prevents recognition of disconfirming evidence needed to reduce epistemic ignorance.

curation_notes.summary: The relation file keeps the strongest current-vocabulary surface from the raw source: outside-view and uncertainty-splitting allies plus the explicit anti-bias tension block.
donor_drops: Did not widen into broader probabilistic graph neighbors because the raw explicit tension block already gives a clear first-order surface.
open_questions: Systems-thinking is a source-real ally, but risk-vs-uncertainty and base-rates were the cleaner bounded anchors.
